title: Been testing a gold EA on Darwinex Zero — interesting behaviour
author: Loose-Object-8913
selftext:
Been running some internal tests on a gold (XAUUSD) EA using a Darwinex Zero style setup (100k, realistic conditions), and noticed something I thought was worth sharing.

The system didn’t trade much at all for a while — which at first felt like it was just missing moves. But looking deeper, it was actually sitting out a lot of messy conditions and only engaging when the structure was clean.

Last week especially was pretty choppy, and it completely avoided it. The week before that it caught a cleaner move.

It’s made me rethink something slightly — less trades doesn’t necessarily mean worse performance. In a lot of cases it’s just better filtering. I also found a filter that makes the EA participant in more bearish movement! Made entries a lot more fun to experiment. 

Any questions ask away!
